name: Baked Pork Chops and Corn
recipeInstructions:
Here's an old-time idea that's probably "Mid-Western" enough for you. This is something I picked up from my ex-Mennonite grandmother in Hutchinson, Kansas.
Take four nice thick pork chops and brown them on each side. Dice up four slices of stale bread (or bread dried out in the oven). Mix that into a can or so of creamed corn. How many cans you need will vary with the brand of corn. Add about half a chopped-up yellow onion and a couple of beaten eggs. Mix the mixture well and add salt and pepper to taste.
Put the browned chops in a greased baking dish, pour the corn mixture over the chops, and pop them in a 325°F oven for about an hour. The corn topping should come out browned and fairly dry, and the chops should be nice and succulent with a hint of sweetness from the corn. This is one dish that garlic will absolutely ruin! I've tried all sorts of ways to fancy up this dish but have never been successful in improving the original simple recipe. And there's not ONE squid tentacle in the whole mess!
Posted by Stephen Ceideburg; March 3, 1991.
